canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, FGVC-Aircraft is consistently defined as a dataset or benchmark for fine-grained visual classification of aircraft. The dataset is specified as containing images of numerous aircraft models, with one paper noting it includes "over 100 different aircraft model variants." Its most prevalent application is as a downstream dataset to evaluate model performance on fine-grained classification, and it is sometimes studied alongside other fine-grained datasets like CUB-200 and Stanford Dogs. While all sources agree on its core purpose, its specific use varies slightly. For example, one study employs it to evaluate "few-shot learning performance," whereas another uses it to assess both "visual classification and explanation quality."
papers: Analyzing and Boosting the Power of Fine-Grained Visual Recognition for Multi-modal Large Language Models
Enhancing Cognition and Explainability of Multimodal Foundation Models with Self-Synthesized Data
Exploring Learning Complexity for Efficient Downstream Dataset Pruning
Weighted Multi-Prompt Learning with Description-free Large Language Model Distillation

definitions.text: A dataset for fine-grained classification of aircraft models, used to evaluate few-shot learning performance.
definitions.paper: Weighted Multi-Prompt Learning with Description-free Large Language Model Distillation

definitions.text: A fine-grained aircraft classification benchmark used to assess visual classification and explanation quality.
definitions.paper: Enhancing Cognition and Explainability of Multimodal Foundation Models with Self-Synthesized Data

definitions.text: A fine-grained visual classification dataset containing images of over 100 different aircraft model variants.
